jokes.text: The last time I said that was when my pet gnus learned the true meaning of Christmas.
jokes.setup: Tracy responding to 'wonderful news' with an example of when he last said that
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of pet gnus (wildebeest) having a Christmas revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tracy moment - the homophone 'gnus/news' plus the impossible scenario of wildebeest having spiritual epiphanies. Classic 30 Rock surreal logic.

jokes.text: Classic Tracy... You'll miss this.
jokes.setup: Tracy makes an absurd statement
jokes.punchline: Tracy immediately commenting on his own comedic value to other characters
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Tracy being a comedic character, with self-aware breaking of fourth wall that's very 30 Rock.

jokes.text: He's a three-year-old boy from Transylvania. Stay with me. His name is Dracul. Hear me out. He is a hemophiliac with a sleep disorder.
jokes.setup: Liz describing a potential adoptee with increasingly vampire-like characteristics
jokes.punchline: The escalating realization that they're essentially adopting a vampire child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke where each detail makes the vampire connection more obvious. The 'stay with me/hear me out' beats show Liz knows how crazy it sounds.

jokes.text: I'm spontaneous. For instance, I started talking before I had an example of how I'm spontaneous.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming she's spontaneous
jokes.punchline: Her only example proves the opposite - she's so planned she notices when she doesn't have a plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character comedy - her neurotic overthinking defeats her attempt to prove she's spontaneous. Self-defeating logic is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: dear doritos, what about just selling bags full of your dust? I could put it on chicken or fish.
jokes.setup: Jack reading a misdirected note meant for Doritos
jokes.punchline: The oddly practical suggestion of selling just the seasoning dust
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about everyone wanting Doritos seasoning. Not exceptional but relatable and well-delivered.

jokes.text: Then I have to drive around with my headlights off until someone flashes me. Then I have to... Well, it's not important.
jokes.setup: Jack describing CEO initiation process
jokes.punchline: What sounds like gang initiation rituals for corporate executives
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of corporate culture with street gang rituals. The cutoff adds mystery and makes it funnier.

jokes.text: She is the only person in the world to have sarcastic last words. 'I just want you to be "happy"." Mother, how I "miss" you.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his mother's final words
jokes.punchline: Even in death, his mother was being sarcastic, and Jack responds in kind
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing the toxic mother-son relationship. The air quotes in death and Jack's matching response is perfectly crafted character work.

jokes.text: Okay, I am not some kind of "nerdery" slut. I like Star Wars.
jokes.setup: Jack offering Harry Potter VIP passes
jokes.punchline: Liz's oddly specific denial and the contradiction of claiming not to be nerdy while citing Star Wars
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Liz's defensive nerdiness. 'Nerdery slut' is a funny coinage, though the Star Wars contradiction is somewhat obvious.

jokes.text: To that store that sells long-sleeve S.P.F. shirts!
jokes.setup: Liz getting excited about going to Florida
jokes.punchline: Her excitement is about sun protection clothing, not typical vacation activities
jokes.explanation: Solid character work showing Liz's practical, unsexy priorities. Not groundbreaking but fits her character well.

jokes.text: No, you need a nobody to sign for that water. We're on TV.
jokes.setup: Delivery person asking for somebody to sign
jokes.punchline: Tracy's egotistical wordplay turning 'somebody' into 'nobody'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Tracy's ego. The pun works and fits his character's inflated sense of importance.

jokes.text: I saw him pack a wig.
jokes.setup: Explaining Pete went to a line producers' convention
jokes.punchline: The random, unexplained detail about the wig
jokes.explanation: Perfect 30 Rock non-sequitur. The unexplained wig detail is economical and creates intrigue about Pete's secret life.

jokes.text: I'm Martin Lutherking from NBC's in-house counsel.
jokes.setup: Lawyer introducing himself
jokes.punchline: The name 'Martin Lutherking' as one word, making MLK into a surname
jokes.explanation: Clever name joke that's both absurd and plausible. Works as a one-off character bit but not deeply integrated.

jokes.text: Hold my calls, giant bee.
jokes.setup: Tracy needs to focus on the lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Addressing Kenneth as 'giant bee' for no explained reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdism. The random 'giant bee' description is economical and creates a funny mental image of Kenneth.

jokes.text: - Wassername! - What's her name?
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions the plaintiff's name
jokes.punchline: Tracy and Jenna's confused responses show they think 'Wassername' is literally her name
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Tracy and Jenna's obliviousness. The confusion feels natural for these characters.

jokes.text: The baby shoe she found after that fire. God, she loved looting.
jokes.setup: Martha showing Jack his mother's possessions
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing Colleen stole from disaster victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revealing Colleen's character through casual admission of disaster profiteering. Martha's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Her nickname here was Mrs. Silly.
jokes.setup: Martha describing the 'happy' Colleen that Jack never knew
jokes.punchline: The completely contradictory nickname for someone Jack knew as miserable
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony showing how differently Colleen behaved with Martha. The nickname is so opposite to Jack's experience it's absurd.

jokes.text: Next, we'll be holding hands in adjacent bathtubs. Or maybe that was a Cialis commercial... it's all a blur.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining this is a scam he's seen before
jokes.punchline: Confusing Dateline episodes with Cialis commercials
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about how crime shows and pharmaceutical ads blend together. The absurd image makes it memorable.

jokes.text: If this is regarding an anaconda in a crawl space, press 1. If a sinkhole full of Indian bones has appeared in your living room, press 2. If you want to know why JAG wasn't on this week, press 3.
jokes.setup: Calling Florida emergency services
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd and Florida-specific emergency options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating Florida stereotypes ending with the random JAG reference. Perfect 30 Rock absurdist humor.

jokes.text: Why can't we just cut this state adrift and let it crash into Cuba?
jokes.setup: Frustration with Florida's insanity
jokes.punchline: The violent solution of literally cutting Florida away
jokes.explanation: Good Jack frustration moment with geographic hyperbole. Not groundbreaking but well-delivered.

jokes.text: What kind of dinosaur was your grandfather?
jokes.setup: Lawyer wants them to start from the beginning
jokes.punchline: Tracy's interpretation of 'beginning' goes back millions of years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic taking 'start from the beginning' to its absurd extreme. Shows his unique thought process.

jokes.text: She's lying like a rug. 'Rug' is an offensive term for Persians that I made up.
jokes.setup: Using the expression 'lying like a rug'
jokes.punchline: Tracy's made-up ethnic slur that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Tracy's absurd logic about creating offensive terms. The randomness works for his character but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: Maybe that tall Asian one. Yeah, Miranda. Her hair is so thick and black.
jokes.setup: Denying they'd harass pages
jokes.punchline: Immediately describing inappropriate thoughts about a specific page
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy showing their immediate contradiction. Works for character but the harassment angle makes it uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I once played a lawyer in a movie, so I know all about winning your son's love back thanks to a magic camera.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming legal expertise from acting
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous movie plot that has nothing to do with law
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy logic connecting acting in bad movies to real expertise. The absurd plot summary is perfect.

jokes.text: And I didn't super-duper finish law school.
jokes.setup: Building tension about the serious lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of being unqualified with childish language
jokes.explanation: Good undermining of authority with the casual 'super-duper.' Shows NBC's incompetence through their lawyer.

jokes.text: I have a gluten thing that turns my business white.
jokes.setup: Jack excusing himself to use the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Graphic overshare disguised as a health concern
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's somewhat cheap. Jack oversharing works for the scene but isn't sophisticated comedy.

jokes.text: Shmesbians!
jokes.setup: Discovering Liz and Jack together
jokes.punchline: Martha's euphemistic made-up word for lesbians
jokes.explanation: Funny euphemism that shows Martha's character. The made-up word is memorable and avoids saying the actual word.

jokes.text: Where are my manners? This is Florida. Let me boil up a pot of hot gatorade. Is blue okay?
jokes.setup: Martha offering hospitality
jokes.punchline: Hot Gatorade as a Florida equivalent of tea
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Florida stereotype joke. Hot Gatorade as hospitality beverage is absurd and perfectly regional.

jokes.text: But they weren't lesbians. My God, of course they were!
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning her aunt and roommate
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate realization that Liz has been oblivious to obvious relationships
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's naivety. Jack's reaction helps the audience realize what Liz missed.

jokes.text: Abraham Lincoln and his roommate, Joshua fry speed, slept in the same bed together until Joshua died from dancing too long at a party.
jokes.setup: Jack defending his mother sharing a bed with Martha
jokes.punchline: The absurd death by excessive dancing as historical fact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical absurdism. The dance death is so ridiculous it might be true, which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I DVR'd a documentary about the woman who designed all of pat summitt's blazers.
jokes.setup: Martha trying to entertain guests
jokes.punchline: The incredibly niche documentary topic
jokes.explanation: Perfect specificity that feels real. The ultra-niche topic is exactly what someone would actually watch.

jokes.text: In some ways, I'm still pinned under a passed-out Harvey Weinstein, and it's Thanksgiving.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining how former lovers can have a hold
jokes.punchline: The disturbing but absurd image of being trapped under Weinstein during a holiday
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's uncomfortable but absurd enough to work. The Thanksgiving detail makes it more surreal.

jokes.text: Sloppy Rodney? It's me, Richard Drench. I remember that ass.
jokes.setup: Hidden camera catching Hazel's reaction to the gigolo
jokes.punchline: Hazel immediately recognizing and propositioning him despite her lawsuit
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Hazel's hypocrisy. The immediate recognition and crude comment works for the plot.

jokes.text: For example, there isn't a hole in my pocket I keep touching my penis through. See? Easy.
jokes.setup: Tracy demonstrating how easy lying is
jokes.punchline: Using an obviously true gross habit as his example of lying
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where Tracy's 'lie' is obviously true. Shows his lack of self-awareness perfectly.

jokes.text: What's the problem, Lemon? They're just lumpy flowers.
jokes.setup: Liz reacting to Martha's pottery
jokes.punchline: Jack's dismissive description of what are clearly phallic sculptures
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to obvious innuendo. Jack's obliviousness or denial creates the humor.

jokes.text: You know what Plato did have? A gay relationship with Socrates.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning their platonic relationship
jokes.punchline: Liz turning classical philosophy into gay history
jokes.explanation: Decent historical reference that fits Liz's theory. Not groundbreaking but works in context.

jokes.text: Finding hookers. Is that right?
jokes.setup: Liz asking Siri to find nearest hotel
jokes.punchline: Siri's misinterpretation of 'hotel' as 'hookers'
jokes.explanation: Standard technology misunderstanding joke. Gets a laugh but isn't particularly creative or well-integrated.

jokes.text: Your pajamas have a turtleneck.
jokes.setup: They're preparing to sleep in the same bed
jokes.punchline: Jack's absurdly formal/modest pajamas
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag showing Jack's uptight nature even in sleepwear

jokes.text: They're from the L.L. Bean maritime professional collection.
jokes.setup: Liz comments on his turtleneck pajamas
jokes.punchline: Overly specific product line that sounds made up
jokes.explanation: Jack's defensive explanation with absurdly specific product branding

jokes.text: Face-to-face, or butt-to-butt?
jokes.setup: Deciding how to sleep in the same bed
jokes.punchline: Jack's business-like approach to the awkward sleeping arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Jack treating intimacy like a business decision. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: There was a particularly youth-oriented priest in my childhood parish who went after everybody but me... Even fat Ralph, and he ate his boogers. I felt so unpretty.
jokes.setup: Jack understanding Liz's question about why nothing happened between them
jokes.punchline: The dark revelation about childhood abuse and feeling left out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revealing Jack's twisted psychology. The fact that he felt rejected by a predator is perfectly disturbed character work.

jokes.text: I've never been high, except for that choir trip to Montreal where I accidentally ate a pound of marijuana.
jokes.setup: Liz listing things she's never done
jokes.punchline: The massive exception that contradicts her claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - the innocent circumstances and massive quantity create great contrast. 'A pound' is an absurd amount.

jokes.text: First of all, it's 'champing' at the bit. Horses champ.
jokes.setup: Liz using the common phrase 'chomping at the bit'
jokes.punchline: Jack correcting her even during an argument in his dead mother's bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his compulsive pedantry even in inappropriate situations. Shows his inability to let things go.

jokes.text: And I know those pots aren't flowers. They're my mother's vagina.
jokes.setup: Jack finally acknowledging what Martha's pottery represents
jokes.punchline: The horrifying realization about his mother's sex life made physical
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the pottery setup. Jack's horrified realization is economically delivered and perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I've seen dresses that look fly, but never dresses that can fly.
jokes.setup: Tracy making a joke about Bjork's swan dress
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'fly' meaning cool vs. literally flying
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay but not particularly original or character-specific

jokes.text: I forgot to make an opening at the end of the slide.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentioning his 1997 water park conviction
jokes.punchline: The hilariously deadly design flaw
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about Tracy's incompetence causing deaths. The casual delivery of such a horrific mistake is perfect.

jokes.text: Colleen's myna bird told me you spent the night in the car.
jokes.setup: Martha greeting Jack in the morning
jokes.punchline: She's getting information from a bird
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke about Colleen's spy bird, now being useful to Martha.

jokes.text: The first three died falling off the car during 'Summer lovin''.
jokes.setup: Martha explaining Colleen was the fourth Sandy in Grease
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of multiple deaths in community theater
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about Florida community theater being deadly. The casual delivery makes the deaths funnier.

jokes.text: I'm in Harry Potter world jail.
jokes.setup: Liz calling Jack for help
jokes.punchline: The existence of theme park jail and Liz ending up there
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to Liz's attempt at spontaneity. 'Harry Potter world jail' is absurd and perfectly specific.

jokes.text: Want to see a porno my cousin is in?
jokes.setup: Demonstrating workplace inappropriateness
jokes.punchline: Jenna's casual offer to share family pornography
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing workplace inappropriate behavior. Fits Jenna's character but isn't particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: I'm just trying to lick it off.
jokes.setup: Kenneth has something in his teeth
jokes.punchline: Jenna's inappropriate solution
jokes.explanation: Simple inappropriate humor, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I came here without studying the park map beforehand, which is how I ended up in a long line that turned out to just be a crowd of people watching two owls have sex.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her spontaneous theme park adventure
jokes.punchline: The absurd reality of what she thought was a ride line
jokes.explanation: Excellent Liz moment showing how her careful planning prevents exactly this kind of mistake. The owl detail is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I just let him read his Hustlers without ever telling him I was a fan.
jokes.setup: Liz listing her regrets
jokes.punchline: Missing the chance to connect with Richard Grieco over pornography
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's specific regrets and social awkwardness. Richard Grieco reference dates it but works.

jokes.text: I know, and a woman of color too... good for her.
jokes.setup: Jack expressing disbelief about his mother
jokes.punchline: Liz thinking he's amazed about the interracial aspect rather than the happiness
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Liz's progressive assumptions missing Jack's emotional point. Well-structured misunderstanding.

jokes.text: My mother died while naming me.
jokes.setup: Correcting Liz about her name being Beverly
jokes.punchline: The tragic explanation for the shortened name
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy in a single line. The economy and tragic absurdity work perfectly for a minor character.

jokes.text: Why don't you discuss it with that little action figure you're married to?
jokes.setup: Bev suggesting they discuss adopting two kids
jokes.punchline: Casual insult about Criss's size
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult with specific action figure comparison, though not particularly original

jokes.text: Next Friday is your last show.
jokes.setup: Hank delivering good news about Liz's glow
jokes.punchline: The devastating news that cancels the show
jokes.explanation: Strong dramatic beat using misdirection. Not a traditional joke but creates impact through structure and timing.

jokes.text: Kenneth doesn't have different hair anymore.
jokes.setup: Tracy announcing good news to Liz
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is Kenneth's hair returning to normal
jokes.explanation: Tracy's typical misplaced priorities - focusing on hair while TGS is cancelled

jokes.text: His body is just a smooth beige tube with a head.
jokes.setup: Hazel describing seeing Kenneth naked
jokes.punchline: The alien description of Kenneth's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual description that confirms Kenneth's non-human nature. 'Smooth beige tube' is perfectly unsettling and memorable.

jokes.text: There were these two African-American giants that were always pressuring me to join their book club.
jokes.setup: Hazel describing harassment at TGS
jokes.punchline: The 'harassment' was being invited to a book club
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor where intellectual invitation is portrayed as harassment
